message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first heartbeat timeout in distributed agent (tick 286, 100-tick milestone)

Migrates the heartbeat-interval timeout in `distributed_agent_stream_session`
(main.rs L14221) to `timeout_with_cx`:

- `runtime_compat::timeout(wait_duration, subscriber.recv())` →
  `timeout_with_cx(&heartbeat_cx, wait_duration, subscriber.recv())`

The distributed agent streams events to an aggregator, waiting up to
`next_heartbeat - now` between events. A timeout triggers a keepalive
heartbeat; an event triggers a payload send. Previously the wait was
cx-insensitive — a cx-cancel at the outer session task could not
propagate through the timeout future.

Now the `_with_cx` variant threads cx through both the timeout budget
calc and the inner future poll, so cx-cancel aborts the wait cleanly
at the next checkpoint rather than letting the timeout run out. This
is valuable because heartbeat intervals can be long (30s+) and the
session task may need to terminate promptly on upstream cancel.

## Tick 286 — 100-tick milestone this session (187-286)

Over 100 ticks of methodical cx-first propagation:
- main.rs cross-crate: 181 sites (storage + wezterm + caut + approval
  + snapshot + replay + simulate + distributed agent + IPC + metrics
  server + diagnostic + secret-scan + workflow + runtime_compat poll
  sleeps + heartbeat timeouts)
- frankenterm-core internal: 47 sites (runtime/mcp/tui/web/mcp_tools/
  workflows/tx_execution/simulation/wait_execution/wezterm trait ext/
  recording/wezterm poll helpers)

Grand total: **228 cx-first migrations**.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 100 (187-286).
main.rs cross-crate migration total: 181 sites (180 before + 1).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 47 (unchanged this tick).
